etymology.description: Originally a pictogram of a man carrying something on their shoulder -> some variants as phono-semantic compound with semantic 人(人) (rén, "person") + phonetic 戈(戈) (gē, "dagger-axe") or 可(可) (kě, "can") -> 現 modern form derives from 人 + phonetic 可
etymology.interpretation: 何 depicts a person, 人(人) (rén, "person"), potentially carrying a 戈(戈) (gē, "dagger-axe") to convey the act of carrying. The phonetic aspect with 戈 indicates the sound, while 可(可) (kě, "can") in later forms reinforces the phonetic component. The idea of 'what' or 'which' extends from the nature of indicating or querying involvement or carrying of objects, as a question of identity or role.

examples.chinese: 你为何不参加会议(你為何不參加會議)？
examples.english: Why are you not attending the meeting?

examples.chinese: 何处可以找到这本书(何處可以找到這本書)？
examples.pinyin: Héchù kěyǐ zhǎodào zhè běn shū?
examples.english: Where can I find this book?
